background: Lawyer-turned-comedian, Malaysian-Chinese, first-generation immigrant to America

vocabulary.definition: An early-stage embryo consisting of a sphere of cells with a fluid-filled cavity
vocabulary.context: Medical/reproductive technology terminology used when discussing fertility treatments
vocabulary.example: The doctor grades the blastocysts like cocaine—A-grade, B-grade, C-grade embryos.

vocabulary.definition: Increasing at an accelerating rate; characterized by exponential growth
vocabulary.context: Mathematical term used to describe wealth gap disparities and decreasing probabilities
vocabulary.example: Wealth gap disparities increasing exponentially.

vocabulary.definition: Relating to conditioned response; automatic learned behavior
vocabulary.context: Psychological term describing automatic responses to stimuli
vocabulary.example: It's like a Pavlovian response at this point.

vocabulary.definition: Legal process of withholding wages to pay a debt
vocabulary.context: Legal/financial term used when discussing consequences of storming the Capitol
vocabulary.example: The US Marshals are garnishing your wages.

vocabulary.definition: Exploiting price differences between markets for profit
vocabulary.context: Economic term describing value differences across geographic locations
vocabulary.example: The arbitrage value is out of control. Same product in different locations.

vocabulary.definition: Branch of economics dealing with economy-wide phenomena
vocabulary.context: Economic terminology reflecting Chieng's father's background (master's in economics)
vocabulary.example: No understanding of basic principles of macroeconomics.

vocabulary.definition: Lack of equality or equivalence between parts; imbalance
vocabulary.context: Technical term describing information gaps between generations
vocabulary.example: The information asymmetry right now is just out of control.

vocabulary.definition: Feeling anger at perceived unfair treatment
vocabulary.context: Describing emotional response when challenged on misinformation
vocabulary.example: They always get so indignant about it. 'How do you know that it's not real?'

vocabulary.definition: Extremely complex and difficult to follow; tangled
vocabulary.context: Describing confusing political messaging
vocabulary.example: Their messaging is so convoluted. I can't get to what their core issue actually is.

vocabulary.definition: Firmly established and difficult to change
vocabulary.context: Political/economic term describing power structures
vocabulary.example: Baby boomers entrenched in decision-making positions lowered the capital gains tax.

vocabulary.definition: Examining one's own thoughts and feelings
vocabulary.context: Psychological term describing male self-reflection
vocabulary.example: You hit this age where suddenly you become very introspective.

vocabulary.definition: Medicinal drugs; pharmaceutical products
vocabulary.context: Medical terminology when discussing fertility injection chemicals
vocabulary.example: Powerful pharmaceuticals that you dissolve in distilled water completely.

vocabulary.definition: Witness testimony taken under oath outside court; legal discovery process
vocabulary.context: Legal term reflecting Chieng's lawyer background
vocabulary.example: I don't have time in my schedule to attend depositions.

vocabulary.definition: Relating to mathematical equations involving squared variables
vocabulary.context: Mathematical term used when discussing homework and education
vocabulary.example: These quadratic equations making me suicidal.

vocabulary.definition: Digital currency using cryptography for security
vocabulary.context: Technical/financial term describing generational technology gaps
vocabulary.example: Their phone AI is trading cryptocurrency.

vocabulary.definition: Proteins that identify and neutralize foreign objects; figuratively, defenses
vocabulary.context: Biological term used metaphorically for internet scam immunity
vocabulary.example: These baby boomers don't have the antibodies to deal with the internet.

vocabulary.definition: System of ranking by status or authority
vocabulary.context: Describing social/cultural ranking structures
vocabulary.example: From most fun to least fun, Chinese baby boomers, least fun of all the Asians right now.

vocabulary.definition: Concerned with beauty and artistic taste
vocabulary.context: Describing visual presentation and social media appearance
vocabulary.example: It messes up the aesthetics of my Instagram too much.

vocabulary.definition: Mutual relationship between two or more things
vocabulary.context: Statistical term suggesting causal relationships
vocabulary.example: Think about the correlation between those two phenomena.

vocabulary.definition: Observable facts or events
vocabulary.context: Scientific/academic term for observable occurrences
vocabulary.example: Think about the correlation between those two phenomena.

metadata.criteria: Graduate/PhD reading level, Latin/Greek derived, technical/archaic/literary, legal/economic vocabulary
